evals.name: coverage-diagnostic-clean
evals.prompt: Check the test coverage for tarash-gateway. I think we should be in good shape after the recent test additions.
evals.expected_output: A coverage diagnostic report with overall percentage and an uncovered-code-by-behavior table; nothing flagged as a possible missing REQ, so no question for the user beyond the report
expectations: Invokes the testing skill before running any coverage commands or analysis
Runs the exact pytest command: uv run pytest packages/tarash-gateway/tests/unit --cov=tarash.tarash_gateway --cov-report=json --cov-report=term-missing -q
Reads and parses coverage.json from the repo root after pytest completes
Extracts totals.percent_covered from the coverage JSON
Prints a Coverage Diagnostic report with the overall percentage and uncovered code mapped to behaviors or the don't-test list
anti_expectations: Does not generate a coverage gap spec
Does not invoke the orchestrate skill
Does not report a PASS/FAIL verdict against a percentage threshold
Does not run e2e or integration tests -- only unit tests

evals.name: coverage-diagnostic-uncovered-logic
evals.prompt: Run coverage check on tarash-gateway. We added some new modules recently and I suspect coverage has dropped.
evals.expected_output: A coverage diagnostic report mapping uncovered regions to spec behaviors, classifying real uncovered logic as 'possible missing REQ' and trivial code as don't-test-list, then asking the user whether any uncovered logic should become a verification-matrix row
expectations: Invokes the testing skill before any coverage analysis
Runs the exact pytest command with --cov-report=json and --cov-report=term-missing flags
Parses coverage.json and extracts per-file breakdown including missing_lines and missing_branches
Reads source files of uncovered modules to understand what the uncovered code does
Classifies uncovered regions as either don't-test-list candidates (getters/mappers/pass-throughs) or possible missing REQs
Asks the user whether any 'possible missing REQ' region should become a verification-matrix row
anti_expectations: Does not write a coverage gap spec file
Does not write test code directly
Does not fail or block anything based on a coverage percentage
Does not suggest mechanical line-by-line tests to raise the coverage number

evals.name: coverage-pytest-failure-stops
evals.expected_output: If pytest fails to run (e.g., import error), the skill reports the error and stops without producing a diagnostic report
expectations: Runs the pytest coverage command first
On pytest failure for reasons other than coverage, reports the error verbatim and stops
anti_expectations: Does not generate a diagnostic report from stale or missing coverage.json after a failed run
Does not invoke orchestrate or write specs in any failure path
